_comment: Hybrid Execution Strategy - Automatically parallelize independent branches while respecting dependencies
description: Hybrid execution automatically analyzes the workflow graph and parallelizes independent branches while maintaining dependencies.
use_cases: Workflows where manual optimization is difficult
disadvantages: Harder to predict exact execution order
May not be optimal for all cases

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of agents to run concurrently across all stages

dependency_resolution.description: How to resolve agent dependencies (automatic = analyze graph, manual = use specified stages)

optimization_level.description: Optimization goal: latency (max parallelism), balanced (moderate), resource (conservative)

timeout_per_agent.description: Timeout in seconds for each agent execution

cycle_detection.description: Detect and handle cycles in the workflow graph

adaptive_concurrency.description: Dynamically adjust concurrency based on system load

latency.description: Maximize parallelism to minimize total execution time
latency.use_when: Low latency is critical and resources are abundant

balanced.description: Balance between latency and resource usage

resource.description: Minimize resource usage, accept higher latency

example_workflow.total_time: time(planner) + max(time(collector_1), time(collector_2)) + time(validator) + max(time(processor_1), time(processor_2)) + time(synthesizer)

dependency_analysis.description: The hybrid strategy performs topological sort to identify execution stages
dependency_analysis.algorithm: Modified Kahn's algorithm with parallel stage detection
steps: 3. For each stage, identify nodes whose dependencies are satisfied
4. Group independent nodes into parallel execution stages
5. Repeat until all nodes are scheduled

best_practices: Let the system auto-detect dependencies (use automatic mode)
Set max_concurrent based on available resources
Add explicit dependencies if auto-detection is incorrect
Use cycle detection for workflows with feedback loops
